nodes.title: Project Scaffolding
nodes.description: Bootstrap the full tech stack: Next.js App Router with TypeScript and Tailwind, Convex backend, Clerk and Resend SDKs installed, environment variables documented. No features — just a runnable foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run dev starts without errors
Convex dev server connects
Clerk, Resend, Convex packages installed
.env.local.example documents all required vars
TypeScript and ESLint compile clean

nodes.title: Database Schema
nodes.description: Define all Convex schema tables: users, designs, orders, jerseyRuns, jerseyRunResponses, and intakes. Includes field types, optional fields, and indexes for common query patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 tables defined in convex/schema.ts
Indexes defined for captainId and jerseyRunId
npx convex dev starts without schema errors
Basic queries for each table return expected shape

nodes.title: Authentication Flows
nodes.description: Wire up Clerk authentication: self-registration, invite-link registration (pre-fills order form from intake), Convex user sync via webhook, and admin flag in Clerk privateMetadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Self-registration creates user in Convex users table
Invite link pre-fills order form from intake record
/portal and /admin routes protected by Clerk middleware
Admin flag only readable server-side

nodes.title: Application Shell
nodes.description: Three Next.js App Router layouts: public marketing (nav + footer), authenticated portal (sidebar + user menu), and admin (admin sidebar). Fully responsive, mobile-first, Sidestep brand colors applied.
acceptanceCriteria: Three distinct layouts with correct route groups
All layouts responsive at 375px, 768px, 1280px
Nav active states highlight current route
Sidestep teal and logo applied
Mobile hamburger menu works on portal and admin

nodes.title: Marketing Pages
nodes.description: Hero, customization options, design-to-delivery process, and FAQ sections. Static content replacing the existing brochure site. Placeholder for jersey photos until client provides assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section with CTA linking to intake form
Customization section shows fabric, neckline, sleeve options
Process section shows 3-step flow
FAQ covers minimum order, timeline, design help, Vancouver-only
Responsive at 375px, 768px, 1280px

nodes.title: Pricing Calculator
nodes.description: Interactive public calculator: quantity input, design fee toggle, instant estimate. Pure client-side. Tiers: 1-9=0, 10-25=0, 26-50=5, 51+=0 per jersey. 50 optional design fee. Unit tested at all tier boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 pricing tiers calculate correctly
Design fee toggle adds 50
Output labeled as estimate
Edge cases at tier boundaries pass unit tests
CTA below calculator links to intake form

nodes.title: Public Intake Form
nodes.description: Lead-capture form on the marketing site: name, team, sport, quantity, brief. Saves to Convex intakes table. No account required. Intake ID becomes the invite token for portal registration.
acceptanceCriteria: Form saves to Convex intakes table on submit
All required fields validated before submission
Success confirmation shown after submit
Intake record includes submittedAt timestamp
Mobile responsive

nodes.title: Customer Portal Dashboard
nodes.description: Authenticated portal home: captain's orders list with status chips, designs list, and empty states. Real-time via Convex subscription. Hub for all customer activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows My Orders with customer-facing status chips
Shows My Designs with file count
Empty states with CTAs for new users
Real-time updates without page refresh
Unauthenticated users redirected to sign-in

nodes.title: Design Creation
nodes.description: Design creation form in the portal: title, brief, optional Canva link, multi-file upload (any file type) to Convex storage. Design detail page with download links. Edit mode to add more files.
acceptanceCriteria: Form creates design record in Convex with uploaded file IDs
Files stored in Convex storage and retrievable via URL
Design appears in portal dashboard after save
Design detail shows all files as download links
Upload progress indicator shown

nodes.title: Order Creation
nodes.description: Full order creation form: team name, sport, quantity, jersey style, neckline, sleeve style, own-design checkbox, and design-linking multi-select. Creates order with initial Inquiry internal stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Order created in Convex with all required fields
Initial internalStages set to Inquiry
Captain can link existing designs to the order
Order appears in dashboard with Order Started status chip
Redirects to order detail page after creation

nodes.title: Order Status Tracking
nodes.description: Order detail page in the portal: 8-stage customer-facing timeline, linked designs, order specs. Real-time Convex subscription. Internal stage names never shown to customer.
acceptanceCriteria: 8 customer-facing stages shown as visual timeline
Current stage highlighted, past stages marked complete
Stage derives from internalStages in real-time
Internal stage names not visible to customer
Linked designs shown with download links

nodes.title: Jersey Run Creation
nodes.description: Captain creates a jersey run from the order detail page: jersey sizes, names mode (open/fixed), fixed roster, custom questions, deadline. Generates a shareable public URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Jersey run created with namesMode, sizes, customQuestions, and deadline
Fixed roster allows captain to pre-define names and numbers
Shareable link /run/<id> generated and displayed with copy button
Link is accessible without authentication
Up to 5 custom questions can be added

nodes.title: Jersey Run Public Form
nodes.description: Public fan submission form at /run/<id>: no account required. Adapts to open vs fixed names mode. Warns on blank name or number. Shows closed state when deadline passes or run is closed.
acceptanceCriteria: Accessible at /run/<id> without authentication
Blank name or number triggers warning before submit
Fixed mode shows roster dropdown, open mode shows free text
Submission creates jerseyRunResponse record
Closed state shown when run is expired or closed

nodes.title: Jersey Run Captain Dashboard
nodes.description: Authenticated captain view of their jersey run responses: live table of all submissions, participation count, deadline countdown, and running price estimate. Real-time via Convex.
acceptanceCriteria: Response table shows name, email, size, jersey name/number, custom answers
Participation count displayed prominently
Deadline countdown shows days remaining
Running price estimate shown using pricing tiers
Data updates in real-time without page refresh

nodes.title: Admin Order Overview
nodes.description: Admin-only order list across all customers: sortable table with team name, captain, sport, quantity, internal stage, date. Clicking opens admin order detail with all fields and captain contact info.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin-only route returns 403 for non-admin users
Orders table shows all customers orders with correct columns
Sortable by any column
Admin order detail shows captain email and all order fields
Admin designs list shows all designs system-wide

nodes.title: Admin Stage Management
nodes.description: 14-stage internal checklist on admin order detail: admin checks/unchecks stages with timestamps. Completing a stage triggers real-time customer-facing stage update in the portal.
acceptanceCriteria: All 14 internal stages shown as checklist with timestamps
Checking a stage sets completedAt timestamp
Unchecking clears the timestamp
Customer portal stage updates in real-time after admin checks a stage
Non-admin users cannot call adminUpdateOrderStages mutation

nodes.title: Admin Customer Management
nodes.description: Admin customer list with New badge for recent signups, intake leads list with Send Invite Link button, and inline editing for any order, design, or customer record field.
acceptanceCriteria: Customer list shows all users with registration date and New badge within 7 days
Leads list shows all intake submissions with invite link copy button
Admin can inline-edit any order, design, or customer field
All mutations enforce admin-only access
New self-registered users appear prominently

nodes.title: Jersey Run Deadline Enforcement
nodes.description: Convex daily cron job closes expired jersey runs and sends Resend emails to captain and Sidestep admin. Reusable close logic also wired to manual admin close button in 3-02.
acceptanceCriteria: Cron runs daily and closes all runs where deadline < now
Resend email sent to captain and info@sidestep.design on close
Already-closed runs are skipped
Manual close by admin triggers same emails
Expired runs show closed state on public form

nodes.title: Admin Jersey Run Oversight
nodes.description: Admin page listing all jersey runs system-wide: team, captain, response count, deadline, status. Full response detail view per run. Manual close button reusing the cron closure logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin jersey runs list shows all runs across all customers
Clicking a run shows full response table with all fields
Close run button sets status to closed and sends emails
Closed runs show badge and disabled close button
Non-admin users cannot access

nodes.title: Admin Data Export
nodes.description: CSV export button on admin order detail. Downloads a file with order info and all jersey run responses formatted for supplier handoff. Admin-only API route.
acceptanceCriteria: Export CSV button triggers browser file download
CSV includes all jersey run response rows when run exists
CSV includes single order row when no run exists
Filename is sidestep-order-<team>-<date>.csv
Non-admin request to export API returns 403

nodes.title: Customer Participation History
nodes.description: Participated In section in the portal dashboard: shows jersey runs the customer submitted responses to (matched by email), with team name, jersey details, and submission date. Read-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Participated In section shows on portal dashboard
Entries matched by respondentEmail to logged-in user email
Each entry shows team name, size, jersey name, number, and submission date
Empty state when no participations
Read-only in Phase 1

nodes.title: SEO Performance and Polish
nodes.description: Final pre-launch pass: Next.js metadata on all pages, OG image, Next.js Image optimization, jersey photos added, Lighthouse score >= 85 mobile, axe accessibility audit, zero TypeScript/ESLint errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Every page has unique title and meta description
OG image set on homepage
Lighthouse Performance >= 85 on mobile
Lighthouse Accessibility >= 90 on all public pages
TypeScript compiles clean with zero errors

nodes.title: Registration Email Notification
nodes.description: Resend email to info@sidestep.design when a new captain self-registers without an invite link. Triggered via the Clerk webhook user.created event. Email includes captain name and email address.
acceptanceCriteria: Resend email sent to info@sidestep.design on self-registration
Email includes captain name and email
Only fires for self-registration not invite-link registration
All tests pass

nodes.title: User Sync Architecture Revisit
nodes.description: Revisit the client-side query+mutation pattern for syncing Clerk users into Convex. Current approach runs a Convex query on every page load for authenticated users. Evaluate: Clerk webhook handled entirely as a Convex HTTP action, or a Convex auth trigger, to guarantee sync fires exactly once on sign-up with no client-side overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: Sync fires exactly once on user creation with no per-page-load queries
No unauthenticated mutations callable from outside the system
Solution works in local dev without ngrok or manual steps
Existing users table data preserved

nodes.title: Fix Mobile Responsive Shell
nodes.description: 1-04 shipped with the mobile hamburger and responsive sidebar broken. Tailwind v4 is not emitting any md: utility classes from SidebarShell (md:hidden, md:translate-x-0, md:ml-64, md:pt-0) even though sm: variants and base classes from the same file are generated correctly. Likely a Tailwind v4 + Next.js dev scan staleness issue or a quirk specific to the md: breakpoint. Once that is fixed, also reconsider the breakpoint choice (md vs lg) for tablet behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Hamburger renders and toggles the drawer at 375px and 768px on /portal and /admin
Sidebar fixed at >=1024px (or chosen desktop breakpoint)
All three layouts visually verified in a real browser at 375 / 768 / 1280
Active state highlight visible on current route
No regressions in existing routes

nodes.title: Optional Intake Image Uploads
nodes.description: Let intake-form visitors optionally attach photos or mood-board images (jerseys they like, team colors, inspiration). Files attach to the intake record so design conversations can start with visual context. Anonymous uploads create real abuse risk — open-write storage would let anyone fill our bucket — so the abuse-control approach needs a decision before implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload UI is fully optional — submitting without files works exactly as today
Abuse-control approach decided and documented (e.g. Cloudflare Turnstile, per-IP rate limit, signed upload URLs, or a server-side proxy) before any code lands
Per-file size cap and MIME allowlist (jpeg/png/webp/heic) enforced server-side, not just in the browser
Total per-intake file count and aggregate size capped
Uploaded files linked to the intake record so 2-13 admin view can preview them
Files visible alongside the intake in the admin lead view (2-13)
Mobile responsive — picker works on iOS Safari and Android Chrome
